channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: The Nintendo Switch 2 has a new worst accessory in the Virtual Boy — the best place for it is on your shelf
item.description: The Nintendo Virtual Boy was a flop on its first go around in the 90s, and in 2026, it isn’t any better as a Nintendo Switch accessory.

item.title: U.S. Company Taken Offline by Cyberattack as Revenge for Missile Strike on Iranian School
item.description: Devices were remotely wiped by the hacking group. The company says the attack is now over.

item.title: OpenAI reportedly building a GitHub alternative after saying Microsoft-owned platform is 'not yet meeting our expectations'
item.description: OpenAI is developing a private code repository following frequent GitHub outages, aiming to improve workflow reliability under AI-driven workloads.

item.title: Nuro is testing its autonomous vehicle tech on Tokyo’s streets
item.description: Nuro began testing its self-driving software on public roads in Tokyo, marking the first international expansion for the AV startup.

item.title: I guess this wasn't an Xbox after all
item.description: In 2024, Microsoft caused a lot of head-scratching and general bemusement with the launch of its "This is an Xbox" marketing campaign. Now, though, it appears the quandary over what is and isn't an Xbox has been resolved. Game Developer noticed that the original blog post on Xbox Wire that kicked off the whole affair has been removed. It seems Xbox will be going a new direction with its future promotions. Maybe since the new Project Helix hardware it has in the works is more definite attempt to blur console and PC gaming, "This is an Xbox" might have been truly confusing as a tagline. Maybe wi...

item.title: Google Play is adding new paid and PC games, game trials, community posts, and more
item.description: The updates indicate that Google is focused on transforming Google Play into an enhanced gaming hub.

item.title: One of Grammarly’s ‘experts’ is suing the company over its identity-stealing AI feature
item.description: For months, Grammarly has been using the identities of real people (including us) for its "Expert Review" AI suggestions without getting their permission, and now it's facing a lawsuit from one of the journalists included, as previously reported by Wired. The class-action complaint filed by journalist Julia Angwin on Wednesday alleges that Superhuman violated the […]...

item.title: Ford’s new AI assistant will help fleet owners know if seatbelts are being used
item.description: Ford Pro AI debuted at Work Truck Week in Indianapolis and is now available to all of its U.S.-based Pro telematics subscribers.

item.title: Ride-hailing inDrive acquires Pakistan’s Krave Mart to bolster grocery delivery
item.description: inDrive has closed its acquisition of KraveMart, expanding grocery delivery in a market dominated by Foodpanda.

item.title: Zendesk acquires agentic customer service startup Forethought
item.description: Forethought was years ahead of its time and the 2018 winner of TechCrunch Battlefield.

item.title: Burson's Stellar headphone amps promise head-fi heaven in spacecraft-inspired Star Wars style
item.description: Burson's new Stellar Series promises high-end headphone amplification in a more compact and accessible form

item.title: iPhone Fold rumor: iPad-like multitasking, but no iPad apps and no Face ID
item.description: Apple's rumored foldable iPhone will come with an iPad-style interface that will allow users to view apps side-by-side, according to a report from Bloomberg's Mark Gurman. Gurman also reports that the outer display will be around the size of a small iPhone. The inner display will reportedly come with a wider aspect ratio, similar to […]

item.title: India neobank Fi winds down banking services on its platform
item.description: Fi, founded by former Google Pay executives, is discontinuing banking services over four years after launch.

item.title: The South Carolina Measles Outbreak Is Slowing Down
item.description: The biggest US measles outbreak in decades may be over sooner than expected.

item.title: Iran-linked hackers launch cyberattack against U.S. medtech company Stryker
item.description: Iran-linked hackers have launched a massive cyberattack on a U.S.-based medical technology company as retaliation for the war in Iran.

item.title: Netflix may have paid $600 million for Ben Affleck’s AI startup
item.description: This deal could rank as among the streaming giant's largest acquisitions ever.

item.title: That wild Scarpetta ending, explained
item.description: Who's at the door in the final moments of "Scarpetta" and who is the killer?

item.title: How the Iran War Could Jack Up Prices on Store Shelves
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz, a busy waterway off the coast of Iran, has come to a near standstill. If the war drags on much longer, it could mean higher prices for consumer goods around the world.

item.title: Some of the best horror games ever made are included in Humble’s latest $15 bundle
item.description: Humble has teamed up with Frictional Games for a new bundle of PC games that pushed the horror genre forward. The bundle includes the Penumbra Collectors Pack, all four Amnesia titles (The Dark Descent, A Machine for Pigs, The Bunker, and Rebirth), and Soma, which I consider to be its opus in terms of visual […]

item.title: Iran Includes American Tech Giants on List of New Targets
item.description: State-aligned media released a list naming the offices of Microsoft, Palantir, and more as potential targets of military action.

item.title: Lovable says it added $100M in revenue last month alone, with just 146 employees
item.description: Swedish vibe-coding unicorn Lovable crossed $400 million in annual recurring revenue (ARR) in February.

item.title: 'Creating a fully keyboard-accessible site...isn’t free; it requires a lot of work and knowledge': Microsoft is making it easier to build complex websites without a mouse
item.description: Microsoft introduces focusgroup to simplify keyboard accessibility, helping developers manage complex website navigation without excessive coding or slow load times.

item.title: Grammarly has disabled its tool offering generative-AI feedback credited to real writers
item.description: Superhuman has taken its writing assistant Grammarly on quite the merry-go-round ride regarding its approach to AI tools. In August, the company launched a feature called Expert Review that would offer feedback on your writing, offering AI-generated feedback that would appear to come from a famous writer or academic of note. These recreations were based on "publicly available information from third-party LLMs," which sounds a lot like web crawlers of dubious legality were involved. The suggested experts would be based on the subject matter and could be anyone from great scientific minds to bes...

item.title: iPhone Fold rumors: Everything we know right now, including the leaked design, upgrades, price and more
item.description: Apple still hasn’t confirmed a foldable iPhone, and the company’s latest round of product announcements didn’t change that. Apple launched several new devices last week, but there was still no mention of a folding iPhone. Even so, the steady stream of leaks and analyst reports hasn’t slowed. Over the past few months, supply-chain sources and leakers have continued to sketch out Apple’s possible plans, with most signs still pointing to a launch in the second half of 2026.As with any unannounced Apple hardware, plenty could shift before anything ships. Features can be revised, timelines can move...
